message:
fix(packs/system/services): close safe-pattern bypass via unit names containing status/show/list

Every systemctl safe subcommand (`status`, `show`, `list-units`, `is-*`,
`daemon-reload`, `cat`) used trailing `\b` — so a unit name that
contained the subcommand keyword as a substring would match the safe
pattern and short-circuit destructive rules:

  systemctl stop status-monitor.service   -> matched systemctl-status safe
  systemctl mask show-deps.service        -> matched systemctl-show safe
  systemctl disable list-units.timer      -> matched systemctl-list safe

Changed trailing `\b` to `(?=\s|$)` on all 7 systemctl safe patterns
(including the legacy `service <name> status` form).
